For the fitness component for the 28 day program, the first and foremost thing is gonna be total activity or steps per day. We wanna track that. I suggest setting a minimum of 5,000 to 10,000 steps depending on the client and how active they are. The surprising thing is even people who are coming in and doing your session say you wanted to run this as an internal program, people will do workouts at your facility but the body will regulate and they'll stop being as active other places and actually lose those steps. So you wanna ensure the steps are being tracked as the regular fitness component. Now one thing that's not on the sheet that I would say would be the next step is to also make sure that they're getting their in-gym workouts. So if someone's paying you for a 28 day challenge to come into your facility, I absolutely would be ensuring that you're tracking their attendance, reaching out to them if they miss their days, giving them that side of things but also making sure they get those workouts. And the third bonus piece is in this portal you're gonna find a 28 day workout plan that they can do at home, on their own, at a park, anywhere. And if you wanna give them that next level you can challenge them and check in and say, hey, I've done any of those workouts. Those are all done for you with examples and they'll be in the portal below. But the goal is to make sure that they're getting the activity. That is the most important thing. That along with the nutrition should get them the most results they will need to see in the 28 days. The rest is all about service from you and getting them to feel good in their facility.
